text:
HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY CLINIC - FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Patient: Marisol Fuentes-Delgado
Department: Medical Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Follow-up visit for metastatic triple-negative breast cancer. Patient returns for review of recent restaging CT imaging and to continue systemic therapy management. She also reports ongoing fatigue and right-sided rib discomfort.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Fuentes-Delgado is a 59-year-old Hispanic woman with a history of stage IIB (T2N1M0) triple-negative, grade 3 invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast diagnosed approximately 21 months ago. Her cancer is notable for germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC), high HRD score (62), PTEN loss, MYC amplification, and PD-L1 CPS 28. She underwent neoadjuvant Keytruda plus dose-dense AC followed by dose-reduced weekly PTX (reduced for grade 3 peripheral neuropathy), then left modified radical mastectomy with contralateral prophylactic mastectomy. Surgical pathology showed mixed pathologic response with residual invasive carcinoma (RCB-II, ypT1cN1mi), prompting adjuvant cape (dose-reduced given CrCl and hand-foot syndrome) plus pembro per KEYNOTE-522 framework. She completed 8 cycles of Xeloda and was on Keytruda maintenance when surveillance imaging approximately 3 months ago identified new hepatic lesions (right lobe, segments VI and VII) and a right posterior 8th rib lytic lesion, biopsy-confirmed as metastatic TNBC. Repeat NGS on the metastatic biopsy demonstrated a new acquired AKT1 p.E17K mutation and CCND1 amplification compared to the primary, with maintained BRCA1 biallelic inactivation, HRD-high profile, and PTEN loss.

Given prior anthracycline, taxane, and cape exposure in the neoadjuvant and adjuvant settings, along with germline BRCA1 mutation and HRD-high status, she was initiated on sacituzumab govitecan (Trodelvy) plus Lynparza (Lynparza) as first-line metastatic therapy, enrolled through a clinical trial. UGT1A1 genotyping revealed *28/*28 homozygous status, and the sacituzumab govitecan dose was proactively reduced to 7.5 mg/kg. Despite this, she developed grade 4 febrile neutropenia during cycle 2, requiring a 4-day hospitalization with IV antibiotics (pip-tazo) and G-CSF support; cultures were negative. Sacituzumab govitecan was further reduced to 5 mg/kg per protocol. She has now completed 3 cycles of sacituzumab govitecan/Lynparza and presents today to review restaging imaging and assess toxicity and overall status.

She reports that she is functional at home but notes significant ongoing fatigue that she describes as limiting her ability to perform household tasks and reducing her quality of time with family. She denies fever, chills, or rigors. She reports continued right-sided rib pain, rated 3-4/10 at rest and up to 6/10 with certain movements (reaching, lifting). She is using acetaminophen 1000 mg every 6 hours and ibuprofen 400 mg three times daily around the clock; this provides partial relief. She continues to decline opioid analgesics at this time, citing fear of sedation and constipation. She has mild intermittent loose stools (1-2 per day, grade 1) that she manages with dietary modification and occasional loperamide as needed. No nausea or vomiting. No new rashes, oral sores, or visual changes. She denies dyspnea, cough, or chest pain (important given prior history of radiation pneumonitis, now resolved). She is tolerating Lynparza with mild nausea in the mornings, controlled by taking the medication with food. No new extremity swelling. She acknowledges she has been experiencing moderate emotional distress and anxiety related to the metastatic diagnosis, and she has had one appointment with the oncology social worker and is awaiting her first psychiatry consultation (referred during a prior visit). She is post-menopausal. Libido and sexual function have not been discussed at this visit.

Current oncologic medications:
- Sacituzumab govitecan 5 mg/kg IV days 1 and 8, every 21 days (current cycle)
- ola 300 mg orally twice daily
- Pegfilgrastim (Neulasta) 6 mg SC on day 2 of each cycle
- Levothyroxine 88 mcg orally daily (for Keytruda-related immune hypothyroidism)
- Metformin 1000 mg orally twice daily (type 2 diabetes)
- Lisinopril 20 mg orally daily (hypertension)
- Duloxetine 30 mg orally daily (neuropathic pain and depression)
- Loperamide PRN
- Acetaminophen 1000 mg orally every 6 hours (scheduled)
- Ibuprofen 400 mg orally three times daily with food (rib pain)
- Urea 40% cream topically to hands and feet (residual hand-foot syndrome care)

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: Fatigue grade 2, ongoing. No fever, chills, or unintentional weight loss. Appetite slightly reduced but adequate caloric intake.
Pulmonary: No cough, dyspnea, hemoptysis, or chest tightness. (Prior radiation pneumonitis resolved.)
Cardiac: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema.
Gastrointestinal: Loose stools 1-2 per day, stable. No nausea at this visit. No abdominal pain or hepatomegaly symptoms. No rectal bleeding.
Musculoskeletal: Right posterior rib pain as described above. Bilateral knee stiffness (known osteoarthritis), unchanged. Peripheral neuropathy in fingertips (grade 1, residual from prior PTX).
Dermatologic: No new rashes. Mild residual palmar-plantar skin dryness and peeling from prior hand-foot syndrome, improved with topical therapy.
Neurologic: Persistent grade 1 peripheral sensory neuropathy in fingertips bilaterally, stable. No new neurologic symptoms. No cognitive changes noted by patient.
Endocrine: Hypothyroidism managed on levothyroxine. No symptoms of over- or under-replacement at this time. Diabetes: glycemic control suboptimally discussed at this visit (HbA1c result pending; last was 7.8%).
Psychiatric: Anxiety and moderate depression, ongoing. Awaiting psychiatry consultation. Engaging with oncology social work.
Hematologic: No excessive bruising or bleeding.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital signs: BP 136/84 mmHg, HR 78 bpm, RR 16/min, Temp 36.8°C, SpO2 98% on room air, Weight 87.3 kg (BMI 33.0, essentially stable).
General: Alert, oriented, in no acute distress. Appears mildly fatigued but is interactive and well-groomed. Affect is somewhat subdued.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Alopecia present (total, ongoing). Oropharynx moist, no oral ulcerations or mucositis.
Neck: Supple. No cervical, supraclavicular, or infraclavicular lymphadenopathy palpable bilaterally.
Chest/Breast: Bilateral mastectomy site with tissue expanders in place bilaterally. Left chest wall with post-radiation skin changes: mild hyperpigmentation, slight induration; no erythema, no fluctuance, no signs of infection. No skin nodules or chest wall masses palpable. No axillary lymphadenopathy appreciated bilaterally.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs. No peripheral edema.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles, wheezes, or dullness to percussion. Good air entry throughout.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Liver edge is not palpably enlarged; no hepatomegaly on exam. Spleen not palpable. No periumbilical lymphadenopathy. Bowel sounds normal.
Musculoskeletal: Tenderness to palpation over right posterior 8th rib (approximately 6 cm from posterior midline). No erythema, warmth, or deformity. ROM of right shoulder mildly limited by pain. Bilateral knee exam with minimal crepitus on flexion; no joint effusion.
Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Sensation diminished in fingertips bilaterally (symmetric, consistent with grade 1 peripheral sensory neuropathy). Gait: normal-based, no antalgic gait observed. Reflexes intact.
Skin: No new rash, no palmar-plantar blistering. Mild residual dryness of palms and soles; no skin breakdown. Nails: Bilateral great toe onycholysis, stable/healing.
Lymph nodes: No peripheral lymphadenopathy in accessible chains.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (most recent, obtained prior to today's visit)
CBC: WBC 3.8 x10^9/L (mild leukopenia, improved from nadir), ANC 1.6 x10^9/L (acceptable, consistent with grade 1 neutropenia nadirs between cycles with pegfilgrastim support), Hemoglobin 9.6 g/dL (grade 2 anemia, stable, above transfusion threshold of 7.5), Platelets 188 x10^9/L (normal).
CMP: Sodium 138 mEq/L, Potassium 3.9 mEq/L, Creatinine 1.18 mg/dL (CrCl ~49 mL/min by CKD-EPI, stable), BUN 22 mg/dL, Glucose 138 mg/dL (fasting). ALT 38 U/L (upper limit normal), AST 32 U/L, Alkaline phosphatase 94 U/L (normal), Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL, Albumin 3.6 g/dL.
TSH: 3.2 mU/L (normal on levothyroxine 88 mcg, therapeutic range achieved).
HbA1c: 7.8% (result confirmed; glycemic control suboptimal but stable; ongoing metformin therapy; endocrinology co-management to continue).
LDH: 248 U/L (mildly elevated, non-specific in metastatic setting; previously 211 U/L; modest increase warrants monitoring).
CA 27-29: 68 U/mL (elevated; prior value at time of metastatic diagnosis was 82 U/mL; trending down, consistent with imaging response - used for directional trending only, not as primary response assessment).
Coagulation: PT/INR and PTT within normal limits.

---

IMAGING RESULTS
Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast (obtained prior to today's visit, approximately 3 months after initiating sacituzumab govitecan/ola): The segment VI right hepatic lesion has decreased substantially, from 1.4 cm at baseline to 0.7 cm - roughly a 50% decrease. The smaller segment VII lesion, previously 0.8 cm, is now essentially not reliably measurable and may represent near-complete resolution at that site. No new hepatic lesions have appeared. No pulmonary nodules, pleural effusions, or mediastinal adenopathy. The right posterior 8th rib lytic lesion is stable in size at approximately 1.5 cm, without interval increase and without new adjacent soft tissue mass. No new bone lesions identified on this CT. Post-surgical and post-radiation changes in the left chest wall are stable, with no suspicious soft tissue nodularity or local recurrence. No new sites of disease in the abdomen or pelvis.

Overall interpretation: The liver lesions are clearly shrinking - encouraging, consistent with meaningful treatment activity from the sacituzumab govitecan/Lynparza combination. The rib lesion is holding steady without progression, which is acceptable given that lytic bone lesions can be slow to show change even with effective therapy. We discussed not over-interpreting the stable rib as a lack of response, given the systemic hepatic activity.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. METASTATIC TRIPLE-NEGATIVE BREAST CANCER (TNBC) - GERMLINE BRCA1-MUTATED, HRD-HIGH - FIRST-LINE METASTATIC TREATMENT, PARTIAL RESPONSE
The patient is responding meaningfully to sacituzumab govitecan (Trodelvy) 5 mg/kg plus Lynparza (Lynparza) 300 mg BID. The hepatic lesions, previously the dominant sites of disease, have shown a clear favorable response: segment VI down approximately 50%, segment VII near-resolved. The rib lesion is stable. There are no new metastatic sites. Tumor marker trending downward (CA 27-29 68 from 82). We will continue current regimen.
   - Continue sacituzumab govitecan 5 mg/kg IV days 1 and 8 of each 21-day cycle. Continue pegfilgrastim on day 2 as primary G-CSF prophylaxis given prior grade 4 febrile neutropenia and UGT1A1*28/*28 homozygous genotype.
   - Continue ola 300 mg orally twice daily. Patient tolerating with mild morning nausea well-controlled with food timing. No dose modification needed at this time.
   - Next restaging imaging planned after 3 additional cycles (approximately 9-10 weeks from today), CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast. Consider dedicated bone imaging at that interval given the known rib lesion.
   - Continue to monitor LDH trend; currently only mildly elevated and trending in favorable direction.

2. HEMATOLOGIC TOXICITY - GRADE 2 ANEMIA, GRADE 1 NEUTROPENIA (NADIRS)
Hemoglobin 9.6 g/dL - grade 2 anemia, stable. ANC at this check 1.6 x10^9/L between cycles, acceptable. No transfusion required. ESA not being used per institutional policy in the metastatic setting. Will continue to monitor CBC with each cycle. If hemoglobin drops below 7.5 g/dL, will reassess transfusion. Consider iron studies at next visit to rule out iron deficiency as contributing factor given poor oral intake and GI losses.
   - CBC to be checked day 1 of each cycle before treatment administration and at least once mid-cycle.

3. RIGHT RIB PAIN - KNOWN LYTIC LESION
Right 8th rib lytic lesion confirmed on prior imaging. Pain currently 3-4/10 at rest, up to 6/10 with movement. Patient is managing on scheduled acetaminophen and ibuprofen. However, ibuprofen use is a concern given borderline renal function (CrCl ~49 mL/min) and chronic NSAID exposure. I have had a direct conversation with the patient about this risk. She acknowledges the concern.
   - Will transition from scheduled ibuprofen to low-dose naproxen with close renal monitoring, or alternatively trial celecoxib (COX-2 selective) with nephrology input given CKD risk. Discussed with patient preference to avoid opioids but counseled that if pain increases or becomes unmanageable, tramadol or low-dose oxycodone would be appropriate next steps and are not something to fear.
   - Referred to radiation oncology for consideration of palliative radiation to the right 8th rib (short course, e.g., 8 Gy x1 or 20 Gy in 5 fractions) for pain control. Right rib lesion may be an excellent candidate for a single-fraction palliative RT approach.
   - Bisphosphonate or denosumab (Xgeva) initiated today: Denosumab 120 mg SC every 4 weeks for skeletal-related event prevention in setting of bony metastasis. Calcium 1200 mg and vitamin D 800 IU daily supplementation co-prescribed to prevent hypocalcemia. Baseline calcium and creatinine confirmed before first dose. Dental clearance recommended before denosumab initiation - patient to contact her dentist and we will place a dental referral today.

4. RENAL FUNCTION / DRUG SAFETY MONITORING
CrCl stable at approximately 49 mL/min. No acute changes. Xeloda dose was already adjusted previously; patient is no longer on cape. ola: no dose adjustment needed at CrCl >31 mL/min per label. Metformin: continue at current dose given stable renal function above 45 mL/min; will re-evaluate if CrCl drops further.
   - Renal function to be rechecked at next visit, particularly if NSAID modification is made.
   - Avoid nephrotoxic medications where possible. Patient educated about hydration.

5. HYPOTHYROIDISM - pembro-RELATED (IMMUNE-MEDIATED)
TSH 3.2 mU/L on levothyroxine 88 mcg daily. Well-controlled. No symptoms of hypothyroidism or hyperthyroidism.
   - Continue levothyroxine 88 mcg daily. TSH recheck in 3 months.

6. TYPE 2 DIABETES MELLITUS
HbA1c 7.8% - suboptimal, though the patient has been under significant metabolic stress from therapy. Metformin 1000 mg BID ongoing. Corticosteroids were used in the past (for radiation pneumonitis) but have been discontinued.
   - Endocrinology co-management to continue. Will coordinate with endocrinology team. Goal HbA1c <8% in this setting given life circumstances, age, and comorbidity burden; no immediate medication escalation today but endocrinology to assess whether addition of a GLP-1 agonist (e.g., semaglutide) is feasible and safe in setting of active cancer treatment.

7. HYPERTENSION
BP 136/84 mmHg today. Adequately controlled on lisinopril 20 mg daily. No medication change needed.
   - Continue lisinopril 20 mg daily. Monitor renal function given concurrent denosumab and chronic kidney disease risk.

8. PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY
Grade 1, stable. Residual from prior Taxol (dose had been reduced during neoadjuvant therapy for grade 3 neuropathy). Currently not worsening with sacituzumab govitecan. Duloxetine 30 mg daily ongoing for neuropathic pain and mood.
   - Continue duloxetine 30 mg daily. If depression intensifies or neuropathy worsens, consider uptitrating duloxetine to 60 mg.

9. PSYCHOSOCIAL DISTRESS / DEPRESSION / ANXIETY
Patient has moderate emotional distress in the context of new metastatic diagnosis, ongoing treatment, and physical symptom burden. She has seen oncology social work once and is awaiting psychiatry consultation.
   - Psychiatry consultation to proceed as scheduled - will follow up with scheduling team to ensure appointment is confirmed within 2 weeks.
   - Duloxetine continued as above for both mood and neuropathic pain.
   - Reinforce with patient and family the importance of mental health support. Validated her emotional response as understandable and appropriate. Provided time for open discussion at today's visit.
   - Palliative care consultation initiated today (telephone order placed). The palliative care team will assist with symptom management (pain, fatigue, nausea, GI side effects), emotional support, and goals-of-care planning. This is a proactive, concurrent palliative care model - does not imply hospice or withdrawal of curative-intent treatment.

10. BONE HEALTH / DENOSUMAB
As noted above under rib pain, denosumab 120 mg SC every 4 weeks initiated today. Dental referral placed. Calcium and vitamin D supplementation started. Patient counseled about jaw osteonecrosis risk (rare; estimated <1% in breast cancer setting; linked to invasive dental procedures - patient instructed to inform all dental providers about denosumab use) and hypocalcemia risk (will check serum calcium and magnesium at 2-week follow-up after first dose).

11. TISSUE EXPANDERS / RECONSTRUCTION
Bilateral tissue expanders are in place. Plastic surgery has been following the patient. Exchange to permanent implants had been planned at approximately 6 months post-surgery; this has been deferred given the metastatic diagnosis and active systemic therapy. Will defer indefinitely until disease status is stable and patient is amenable. Will coordinate with plastic surgery for continued surveillance.

12. GERMLINE BRCA1 / FAMILY COUNSELING
Patient has known germline BRCA1 c.5266dupC pathogenic variant. Genetic counseling was completed prior to neoadjuvant initiation. Family members who have not been tested were counseled previously. She opted against ola at the adjuvant stage; however, she is now benefiting from ola in the metastatic setting as part of the current combination regimen. No additional genetic testing warranted at this time.

---

PLAN SUMMARY
- Continue sacituzumab govitecan 5 mg/kg days 1 and 8 with day 2 pegfilgrastim
- Continue ola 300 mg BID
- Initiate denosumab 120 mg SC every 4 weeks; calcium and vitamin D supplementation; dental referral placed
- Palliative care consultation ordered
- Psychiatry consultation to be confirmed within 2 weeks
- Radiation oncology referral for palliative RT to right 8th rib
- Transition scheduled ibuprofen to safer analgesic option given CKD risk (COX-2 inhibitor preferred, nephrology input if needed)
- Continue levothyroxine 88 mcg, metformin 1000 mg BID, lisinopril 20 mg, duloxetine 30 mg
- Labs at next visit (cycle day 1): CBC with differential, CMP, TSH, LDH, CA 27-29, calcium, magnesium
- Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast in approximately 9-10 weeks
- Return to clinic in 3 weeks (cycle day 1 of next cycle) or sooner if new/worsening symptoms

ECOG Performance Status: 1-2
Cancer Status: Metastatic TNBC, partial response to current first-line metastatic therapy. Active disease, on treatment, responding.

Time spent: 55 minutes (>50% counseling and care coordination).

Electronically signed by: [Attending Medical Oncologist]
Department of Medical Oncology
